 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-hnv","title":"FEAT: Project Scaffolding and Monorepo Setup","description":"## Overview

Project scaffolding is the foundational infrastructure that enables all other Flywheel Gateway development. This bead establishes the monorepo structure, build tooling, shared package architecture, and CI quality gates that all Phase 1+ features depend on.

## Background & Reasoning

### Why a monorepo?
- **Atomic changes**: cross-package changes are committed together
- **Shared tooling**: single TypeScript + Biome config, consistent scripts
- **Type safety**: local packages type-check together
- **Dev experience**: fast iteration without publish/install cycles

### Why Bun workspaces?
- **Speed**: fast installs + script execution
- **Native TypeScript**: streamlined dev workflow
- **Workspace support**: first-class monorepo support

### Technology Choices

| Technology | Rationale |
|------------|-----------|
| **Bun** | Runtime + package manager + test runner |
| **TypeScript** | Strict type safety, IDE support |
| **Biome** | Unified linting/formatting |
| **Hono** | Backend HTTP framework |
| **Drizzle** | Type-safe ORM for SQLite |
| **React** | UI framework |
| **Tailwind** | Styling system |
| **TanStack Router/Query** | Routing + data fetching |

## Scope & Requirements

### Monorepo Structure

```
flywheel_gateway/
├── apps/
│   ├── gateway/
│   │   ├── src/
│   │   │   ├── routes/
│   │   │   ├── services/
│   │   │   ├── middleware/
│   │   │   ├── db/
│   │   │   ├── ws/
│   │   │   ├── openapi/
│   │   │   └── index.ts
│   │   ├── tests/
│   │   └── package.json
│   └── web/
│       ├── src/
│       │   ├── components/
│       │   ├── hooks/
│       │   ├── lib/
│       │   ├── pages/
│       │   ├── stores/
│       │   └── main.tsx
│       ├── tests/
│       └── package.json
├── packages/
│   ├── shared/
│   │   ├── src/
│   │   │   ├── types/
│   │   │   ├── schemas/
│   │   │   ├── commands/
│   │   │   └── utils/
│   │   └── package.json
│   ├── agent-drivers/
│   │   ├── src/
│   │   │   ├── sdk/
│   │   │   ├── acp/
│   │   │   ├── tmux/
│   │   │   └── interface.ts
│   │   └── package.json
│   ├── flywheel-clients/
│   │   ├── src/
│   │   │   ├── agentmail/
│   │   │   ├── bv/
│   │   │   ├── cass/
│   │   │   ├── cm/
│   │   │   └── scanner/
│   │   └── package.json
│   └── test-utils/
│       ├── src/
│       │   ├── db.ts
│       │   ├── api.ts
│       │   ├── ws.ts
│       │   ├── agent.ts
│       │   ├── time.ts
│       │   ├── assertions.ts
│       │   ├── logging.ts
│       │   └── index.ts
│       └── package.json
├── tests/
│   ├── e2e/
│   ├── contract/
│   └── load/
├── docs/
│   └── PLAN.md
├── .beads/
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
└── biome.json
```

### Root `package.json` (workspace wiring)

```json
{
  "name": "flywheel-gateway",
  "private": true,
  "workspaces": ["apps/*", "packages/*"],
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "bun dev",
    "dev:gateway": "bun dev:gateway",
    "dev:web": "bun dev:web",
    "build": "bun run --filter '*' build",
    "test": "bun test",
    "test:integration": "bun test --filter 'tests/integration/**'",
    "test:e2e": "bunx playwright test",
    "lint": "bun lint",
    "lint:fix": "bun lint:fix",
    "format": "bun format",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "db:generate": "bun db:generate",
    "db:migrate": "bun db:migrate",
    "db:studio": "bun db:studio"
  }
}
```

### TypeScript configuration

- Strict mode enabled
- Base tsconfig shared across packages
- Workspace path aliases for local packages

### Biome configuration

- Lint + format configured for the repo
- CI runs `biome check .` (no auto-write)

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- [ ] TypeScript compilation succeeds with strict mode
- [ ] All package exports are typed correctly
- [ ] Path aliases resolve correctly
- [ ] Biome linting passes on representative code

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Bun workspaces resolve inter-package dependencies
- [ ] Changes in `packages/shared` reflect in consuming packages
- [ ] `bun dev` starts gateway + web concurrently

### E2E Tests
- [ ] `bun test:e2e` boots the system and validates a minimal smoke flow (gateway health + UI loads)

### Failure Mode Tests
- [ ] Missing dependency / misconfigured workspace fails with actionable error output

### Logging
- [ ] Test logs include a run-level `correlationId` and redact secrets


## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Monorepo structure created with all required directories
- [ ] Root `package.json` configured for Bun workspaces and common scripts
- [ ] Base `tsconfig.json` strict settings applied repo-wide
- [ ] `biome.json` present; `bun lint` and `bun format` are wired
- [ ] Gateway app starts and responds to a health check endpoint
- [ ] Web app starts and renders a placeholder page
- [ ] CI runs: typecheck + lint + unit tests (+ e2e smoke on PR)

## References

- `docs/PLAN.md` (Phases + File Structure + Testing)

 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-i6c","title":"Custom Dashboard Builder","description":"## Background

As the Flywheel Gateway platform matures, users need the ability to create personalized views of their agent ecosystem, costs, and activity metrics. A custom dashboard builder enables users to construct tailored monitoring interfaces without requiring engineering intervention, democratizing data visualization across the organization.

## Problem Statement

Currently, users are limited to predefined dashboard layouts that may not align with their specific monitoring needs. Different roles (developers, managers, finance) have distinct requirements for data visualization. Without customization capabilities, users either lack visibility into relevant metrics or must request engineering resources for custom reports.

## Technical Approach

### Core Architecture

The dashboard builder will leverage react-grid-layout for drag-and-drop widget placement, providing a familiar interface similar to tools like Grafana or Datadog dashboards.

```typescript
// apps/web/src/components/analytics/DashboardBuilder.tsx
interface Dashboard {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  description?: string;
  ownerId: string;
  visibility: 'private' | 'team' | 'public';
  teamId?: string;
  layout: DashboardLayout;
  widgets: Widget[];
  refreshInterval: number; // seconds, 0 = manual only
  createdAt: Date;
  updatedAt: Date;
}

interface DashboardLayout {
  columns: number; // typically 12 or 24
  rowHeight: number;
  margin: [number, number];
  containerPadding: [number, number];
}

interface Widget {
  id: string;
  type: WidgetType;
  title: string;
  position: { x: number; y: number; w: number; h: number };
  config: WidgetConfig;
  dataSource: DataSourceConfig;
}

type WidgetType = 
  | 'metric-card'      // Single KPI with trend
  | 'line-chart'       // Time series
  | 'bar-chart'        // Categorical comparison
  | 'pie-chart'        // Distribution
  | 'table'            // Tabular data
  | 'agent-list'       // Agent status list
  | 'activity-feed'    // Recent events stream
  | 'cost-breakdown'   // Cost visualization
  | 'heatmap'          // Usage patterns
  | 'gauge'            // Progress/capacity
  | 'text'             // Markdown content
  | 'iframe';          // External embeds
```

### Widget Type Specifications

**Metric Cards:**
- Single value display with optional sparkline
- Trend indicator (up/down/neutral with percentage)
- Threshold-based coloring (green/yellow/red)
- Click-through to detailed view

**Charts (Line, Bar, Pie):**
- Built on Chart.js or Recharts
- Configurable time ranges
- Multiple series support
- Legend positioning
- Tooltip customization
- Export as PNG/SVG

**Tables:**
- Sortable columns
- Pagination
- Column visibility toggles
- Row click actions
- Inline search/filter
- CSV export

**Agent Lists:**
- Status indicators (active/idle/error/offline)
- Quick actions (pause, configure, view logs)
- Grouping by team/project/model
- Search and filter

**Activity Feeds:**
- Real-time updates via WebSocket
- Event type filtering
- Expandable details
- Jump to related entity

### Widget Configuration Panel

```typescript
interface WidgetConfigPanel {
  // Common settings
  title: string;
  description?: string;
  refreshInterval?: number;
  
  // Data source
  dataSource: {
    type: 'api' | 'query' | 'static';
    endpoint?: string;
    query?: string; // For custom queries
    filters?: Record<string, any>;
    timeRange?: TimeRange;
  };
  
  // Display settings (type-specific)
  display: {
    colorScheme?: string;
    showLegend?: boolean;
    showGrid?: boolean;
    // ... type-specific options
  };
  
  // Thresholds
  thresholds?: {
    warning?: number;
    critical?: number;
  };
}
```

### Auto-Refresh Configuration

```typescript
interface RefreshConfig {
  globalInterval: number; // Dashboard-wide default
  widgetOverrides: Map<string, number>; // Per-widget overrides
  pauseOnHidden: boolean; // Pause when tab not visible
  pauseOnError: boolean; // Pause widget on consecutive errors
  maxRetries: number; // Before pausing
}
```

### Sharing and Access Control

```typescript
interface DashboardSharing {
  visibility: 'private' | 'team' | 'public';
  teamId?: string; // Required for 'team' visibility
  
  // Granular permissions
  viewers: string[]; // User IDs with read access
  editors: string[]; // User IDs with edit access
  
  // Public options
  publicSlug?: string; // URL-friendly identifier
  requireAuth: boolean; // Require login even for public
  embedEnabled: boolean; // Allow iframe embedding
  embedToken?: string; // For secure embedding
}
```

### Backend Storage

```sql
-- Dashboards table
CREATE TABLE dashboards (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
  org_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES organizations(id),
  owner_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
  name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  visibility VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'private',
  team_id UUID REFERENCES teams(id),
  layout JSONB NOT NULL,
  widgets JSONB NOT NULL,
  refresh_interval INTEGER DEFAULT 60,
  public_slug VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE,
  embed_enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
  embed_token VARCHAR(64),
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Dashboard permissions
CREATE TABLE dashboard_permissions (
  dashboard_id UUID REFERENCES dashboards(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  permission VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL, -- 'view' or 'edit'
  PRIMARY KEY (dashboard_id, user_id)
);

-- Dashboard favorites for quick access
CREATE TABLE dashboard_favorites (
  user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  dashboard_id UUID REFERENCES dashboards(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  PRIMARY KEY (user_id, dashboard_id)
);
```

### API Endpoints

```typescript
// Dashboard CRUD
GET    /api/v1/dashboards                    // List user's dashboards
POST   /api/v1/dashboards                    // Create dashboard
GET    /api/v1/dashboards/:id                // Get dashboard
PUT    /api/v1/dashboards/:id                // Update dashboard
DELETE /api/v1/dashboards/:id                // Delete dashboard
POST   /api/v1/dashboards/:id/duplicate      // Clone dashboard

// Sharing
PUT    /api/v1/dashboards/:id/sharing        // Update sharing settings
GET    /api/v1/dashboards/:id/permissions    // List permissions
POST   /api/v1/dashboards/:id/permissions    // Add permission
DELETE /api/v1/dashboards/:id/permissions/:userId

// Widget data
GET    /api/v1/dashboards/:id/widgets/:widgetId/data  // Fetch widget data

// Public access
GET    /api/v1/public/dashboards/:slug       // Public dashboard view
GET    /api/v1/embed/:token                  // Embedded dashboard
```

## File Locations

- apps/web/src/components/analytics/DashboardBuilder.tsx - Main builder component
- apps/web/src/components/analytics/DashboardGrid.tsx - react-grid-layout wrapper
- apps/web/src/components/analytics/widgets/ - Widget components directory
- apps/web/src/components/analytics/WidgetConfigPanel.tsx - Configuration sidebar
- apps/web/src/hooks/useDashboard.ts - Dashboard state management
- apps/gateway/src/services/dashboard.service.ts - Backend service
- apps/gateway/src/controllers/dashboard.controller.ts - API controller
- packages/shared/src/types/dashboard.ts - Shared type definitions

## Dependencies

- react-grid-layout: ^1.4.0 (drag-and-drop grid)
- recharts: ^2.8.0 (charting library)
- @tanstack/react-query: For data fetching and caching
- date-fns: Time range calculations
- react-markdown: For text widgets

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Users can create, edit, and delete custom dashboards
2. Drag-and-drop widget placement works smoothly on desktop browsers
3. All specified widget types are implemented and functional
4. Widget configuration panel allows customizing all relevant settings
5. Auto-refresh works with configurable intervals (15s, 30s, 1m, 5m, 15m, off)
6. Dashboards can be shared with teams or made public
7. Public dashboards are accessible via slug URLs
8. Dashboard state persists correctly across sessions
9. Performance: Dashboard with 20 widgets loads in < 3 seconds
10. Mobile-responsive layout degrades gracefully
11. Widgets display loading states and error handling
12. Export functionality available for charts and tables

## Testing Strategy

- Unit tests for widget components
- Integration tests for dashboard CRUD operations
- E2E tests for drag-and-drop interactions
- Performance tests for large dashboards
- Cross-browser testing (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)

## Reference

PLAN.md section 21.8 - Custom Dashboard Builder


## Testing Requirements

Reference: `flywheel_gateway-d8b`.

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Dashboard layout serialization/deserialization is stable and backward-compatible within a major version
- [ ] Widget registry rejects invalid configs with actionable errors
- [ ] Grid/layout constraints prevent overlapping/invalid placements

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Create/update/delete dashboard via REST and verify persistence + retrieval

### E2E Tests (UI)
- [ ] Drag/drop widgets, resize, save, reload → layout persists and renders correctly

### Logging
- [ ] Logs include correlationId + dashboardId + widgetCount + action; widget payloads are validated and sanitized

 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-jc8","title":"[Epic] OpenAPI Specification Enhancement","description":"# Epic: OpenAPI Specification Enhancement

## Background & Problem Statement
The codebase has OpenAPI generation capability (`packages/shared/src/commands/codegen/openapi.ts`), but it generates generic schemas instead of specific types derived from the actual Zod validators.

### Current State Analysis

**Current generation (openapi.ts:139-145):**
```typescript
responses: {
  "200": {
    description: "Success",
    content: {
      "application/json": {
        schema: { type: "object" },  // ← Generic!
      },
    },
  },
```

**Request body (openapi.ts:173-180):**
```typescript
requestBody: {
  required: true,
  content: {
    "application/json": {
      schema: { type: "object" },  // ← Generic!
    },
  },
},
```

### Problems
1. **No Schema Detail**: All schemas are just `{ type: "object" }`
2. **No Request Validation Docs**: Zod schemas not reflected
3. **No Response Type Info**: Can't generate client types
4. **Poor DX**: Developers can't see expected request/response shapes

### What We Have (Unused)
Each route file has detailed Zod schemas:

```typescript
// agents.ts
const SpawnRequestSchema = z.object({
  workingDirectory: z.string().min(1),
  agentId: z.string().min(1).optional(),
  systemPrompt: z.string().min(1).optional(),
  timeout: z.number().min(1000).max(86400000).optional(),
  maxTokens: z.number().min(1000).max(1000000).optional(),
});
```

These should be converted to OpenAPI schemas.

### Industry Standard
Proper OpenAPI spec:
```yaml
components:
  schemas:
    SpawnAgentRequest:
      type: object
      required:
        - workingDirectory
      properties:
        workingDirectory:
          type: string
          minLength: 1
        agentId:
          type: string
          minLength: 1
        timeout:
          type: integer
          minimum: 1000
          maximum: 86400000
```

## Goals
1. **Accurate Schemas**: OpenAPI reflects actual Zod validators
2. **Client Generation**: Enable typed client generation
3. **Documentation**: Accurate API docs from spec
4. **Validation Parity**: OpenAPI constraints match Zod

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Zod-to-OpenAPI conversion utility created
- [ ] All request schemas properly documented
- [ ] All response schemas properly documented
- [ ] Error response schemas included
- [ ] OpenAPI spec validates correctly
- [ ] Can generate TypeScript client from spec

## Technical Approach
1. Use `zod-to-json-schema` library or similar
2. Register schemas in central location
3. Reference schemas in operation definitions
4. Include error response schemas
5. Add examples to schemas

## Libraries to Consider
- `zod-to-json-schema`: Direct Zod → JSON Schema
- `@asteasolutions/zod-to-openapi`: Full OpenAPI integration
- Custom conversion for more control

## Schema Categories
1. **Request Schemas**: From route validation schemas
2. **Response Schemas**: Define alongside handlers
3. **Error Schemas**: From error taxonomy
4. **Common Schemas**: Pagination, timestamps, etc.

## Dependencies
- Response Structure Standardization (defines response shape)
- Validation Error Enhancement (error schemas)

## Risks & Mitigations
- **Schema Drift**: Zod and OpenAPI get out of sync
  - Mitigation: Generate OpenAPI from Zod at build time
- **Complexity**: Full OpenAPI is verbose
  - Mitigation: Use JSON refs to reduce duplication","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:01:02.054643751-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T20:47:53.017283065-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T20:47:53.017283065-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented comprehensive OpenAPI schema generation from Zod validators. Added schemas and routes for Dashboards (13 endpoints) and Cost Analytics (15 endpoints). OpenAPI spec now has 40 paths and 94 schemas derived from actual Zod validators.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_gateway-jc8","depends_on_id":"flywheel_gateway-tt0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:14:01.414441013-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_gateway-jc8","depends_on_id":"flywheel_gateway-0ua","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T10:14:01.451862415-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-jjn","title":"RU: REST API Routes","description":"## Problem Statement

The Gateway needs REST API routes for all RU operations: fleet management, sync operations, and agent-sweep workflows.

## Background

Based on the RU service layer, we need endpoints for:
1. **Fleet management** - CRUD for repos, stats, groups
2. **Sync operations** - Start/cancel sync, history, progress
3. **Agent sweep** - Sessions, plans, approval, logs
4. **Status** - Quick health checks for dashboards

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Fleet Management Routes

```typescript
// apps/gateway/src/routes/ru.ts

import { Hono } from "hono";
import { zValidator } from "@hono/zod-validator";
import { z } from "zod";
import {
  getFleetRepos,
  getFleetStats,
  addRepoToFleet,
  removeRepoFromFleet,
  updateRepoStatus,
} from "../services/ru-fleet.service";
import {
  startFleetSync,
  cancelSync,
  getSyncHistory,
} from "../services/ru-sync.service";
import {
  startAgentSweep,
  getSweepSession,
  getSweepSessions,
  approveSweepPlan,
  rejectSweepPlan,
  pauseSweepSession,
  resumeSweepSession,
  cancelSweepSession,
} from "../services/ru-sweep.service";
import { logger } from "../services/logger";

export const ru = new Hono();

// ==================== Fleet Management ====================

// GET /ru/fleet - List repos in fleet
ru.get("/fleet", async (c) => {
  const status = c.req.query("status");
  const group = c.req.query("group");
  const owner = c.req.query("owner");
  const limit = Number(c.req.query("limit")) || 100;
  const offset = Number(c.req.query("offset")) || 0;

  const { repos, total } = await getFleetRepos({ status, group, owner, limit, offset });

  return c.json({
    repos,
    total,
    limit,
    offset,
    hasMore: offset + repos.length < total,
  });
});

// GET /ru/fleet/stats - Fleet statistics
ru.get("/fleet/stats", async (c) => {
  const stats = await getFleetStats();
  return c.json(stats);
});

// GET /ru/fleet/groups - List repo groups
ru.get("/fleet/groups", async (c) => {
  const groups = await getFleetGroups();
  return c.json({ groups });
});

// POST /ru/fleet - Add repo to fleet
const addRepoSchema = z.object({
  owner: z.string().min(1),
  name: z.string().min(1),
  url: z.string().url(),
  sshUrl: z.string().optional(),
  group: z.string().optional(),
});

ru.post("/fleet", zValidator("json", addRepoSchema), async (c) => {
  const body = c.req.valid("json");
  const repo = await addRepoToFleet(body);
  return c.json({ success: true, repo }, 201);
});

// POST /ru/fleet/import - Import repos from GitHub org/user
const importSchema = z.object({
  source: z.enum(["github"]),
  owner: z.string().min(1),
  filter: z.object({
    includePrivate: z.boolean().optional(),
    includeArchived: z.boolean().optional(),
    namePattern: z.string().optional(),
  }).optional(),
  group: z.string().optional(),
});

ru.post("/fleet/import", zValidator("json", importSchema), async (c) => {
  const body = c.req.valid("json");
  const result = await importReposFromGitHub(body);
  return c.json({ success: true, imported: result.imported, skipped: result.skipped });
});

// GET /ru/fleet/:repoId - Get single repo
ru.get("/fleet/:repoId", async (c) => {
  const { repoId } = c.req.param();
  const repo = await getRepoById(repoId);

  if (!repo) {
    return c.json({ error: "Repo not found" }, 404);
  }

  return c.json(repo);
});

// PATCH /ru/fleet/:repoId - Update repo
const updateRepoSchema = z.object({
  group: z.string().optional(),
  ruConfig: z.record(z.unknown()).optional(),
});

ru.patch("/fleet/:repoId", zValidator("json", updateRepoSchema), async (c) => {
  const { repoId } = c.req.param();
  const body = c.req.valid("json");

  await updateRepoStatus(repoId, body);
  return c.json({ success: true });
});

// DELETE /ru/fleet/:repoId - Remove repo from fleet
ru.delete("/fleet/:repoId", async (c) => {
  const { repoId } = c.req.param();

  try {
    await removeRepoFromFleet(repoId);
    return c.json({ success: true });
  } catch (error) {
    if (error instanceof NotFoundError) {
      return c.json({ error: error.message }, 404);
    }
    throw error;
  }
});

// ==================== Sync Operations ====================

// POST /ru/sync - Start fleet sync
const syncSchema = z.object({
  parallelism: z.number().min(1).max(10).optional(),
  dryRun: z.boolean().optional(),
  force: z.boolean().optional(),
  filter: z.object({
    repos: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
    group: z.string().optional(),
    owner: z.string().optional(),
  }).optional(),
});

ru.post("/sync", zValidator("json", syncSchema), async (c) => {
  const body = c.req.valid("json");
  const user = c.get("user") || "anonymous";

  const { sessionId } = await startFleetSync(user, body);

  return c.json({ success: true, sessionId }, 202);
});

// GET /ru/sync/:sessionId - Get sync session status
ru.get("/sync/:sessionId", async (c) => {
  const { sessionId } = c.req.param();
  const operations = await getSyncHistory({ sessionId });

  const stats = {
    total: operations.length,
    pending: operations.filter(o => o.status === "pending").length,
    running: operations.filter(o => o.status === "running").length,
    success: operations.filter(o => o.status === "success").length,
    failed: operations.filter(o => o.status === "failed").length,
  };

  return c.json({ sessionId, stats, operations });
});

// POST /ru/sync/:sessionId/cancel - Cancel sync
ru.post("/sync/:sessionId/cancel", async (c) => {
  const { sessionId } = c.req.param();

  await cancelSync(sessionId);

  return c.json({ success: true });
});

// GET /ru/sync/history - Sync history
ru.get("/sync/history", async (c) => {
  const repoId = c.req.query("repoId");
  const limit = Number(c.req.query("limit")) || 50;

  const history = await getSyncHistory({ repoId, limit });

  return c.json({ history });
});

// POST /ru/sync/repo/:repoId - Sync single repo
ru.post("/sync/repo/:repoId", async (c) => {
  const { repoId } = c.req.param();
  const user = c.get("user") || "anonymous";

  const { sessionId } = await startFleetSync(user, {
    filter: { repos: [repoId] },
  });

  return c.json({ success: true, sessionId }, 202);
});

// ==================== Agent Sweep ====================

// GET /ru/sweep - List sweep sessions
ru.get("/sweep", async (c) => {
  const status = c.req.query("status");
  const limit = Number(c.req.query("limit")) || 20;
  const offset = Number(c.req.query("offset")) || 0;

  const sessions = await getSweepSessions({ status, limit, offset });

  return c.json({ sessions });
});

// POST /ru/sweep - Start new sweep
const sweepSchema = z.object({
  targetRepos: z.union([z.literal("*"), z.array(z.string())]),
  parallelism: z.number().min(1).max(10).optional(),
  dryRun: z.boolean().optional(),
  autoApprove: z.boolean().optional(),
  phase1Timeout: z.number().optional(),
  phase2Timeout: z.number().optional(),
  phase3Timeout: z.number().optional(),
});

ru.post("/sweep", zValidator("json", sweepSchema), async (c) => {
  const body = c.req.valid("json");
  const user = c.get("user") || "anonymous";

  const session = await startAgentSweep(user, body);

  return c.json({ success: true, session }, 202);
});

// GET /ru/sweep/:sessionId - Get sweep session details
ru.get("/sweep/:sessionId", async (c) => {
  const { sessionId } = c.req.param();

  try {
    const session = await getSweepSession(sessionId);
    return c.json(session);
  } catch (error) {
    if (error instanceof NotFoundError) {
      return c.json({ error: error.message }, 404);
    }
    throw error;
  }
});

// POST /ru/sweep/:sessionId/pause - Pause sweep
ru.post("/sweep/:sessionId/pause", async (c) => {
  const { sessionId } = c.req.param();

  await pauseSweepSession(sessionId);

  return c.json({ success: true });
});

// POST /ru/sweep/:sessionId/resume - Resume sweep
ru.post("/sweep/:sessionId/resume", async (c) => {
  const { sessionId } = c.req.param();

  await resumeSweepSession(sessionId);

  return c.json({ success: true });
});

// POST /ru/sweep/:sessionId/cancel - Cancel sweep
ru.post("/sweep/:sessionId/cancel", async (c) => {
  const { sessionId } = c.req.param();

  await cancelSweepSession(sessionId);

  return c.json({ success: true });
});

// GET /ru/sweep/:sessionId/plans - Get plans for session
ru.get("/sweep/:sessionId/plans", async (c) => {
  const { sessionId } = c.req.param();
  const status = c.req.query("status"); // pending|approved|rejected

  const plans = await getSweepPlans(sessionId, { status });

  return c.json({ plans });
});

// GET /ru/sweep/:sessionId/logs - Get logs for session
ru.get("/sweep/:sessionId/logs", async (c) => {
  const { sessionId } = c.req.param();
  const level = c.req.query("level");
  const limit = Number(c.req.query("limit")) || 100;

  const logs = await getSweepLogs(sessionId, { level, limit });

  return c.json({ logs });
});

// POST /ru/sweep/plans/:planId/approve - Approve plan
ru.post("/sweep/plans/:planId/approve", async (c) => {
  const { planId } = c.req.param();
  const user = c.get("user") || "anonymous";

  await approveSweepPlan(planId, user);

  return c.json({ success: true });
});

// POST /ru/sweep/plans/:planId/reject - Reject plan
const rejectSchema = z.object({
  reason: z.string().min(1),
});

ru.post("/sweep/plans/:planId/reject", zValidator("json", rejectSchema), async (c) => {
  const { planId } = c.req.param();
  const { reason } = c.req.valid("json");
  const user = c.get("user") || "anonymous";

  await rejectSweepPlan(planId, user, reason);

  return c.json({ success: true });
});

// POST /ru/sweep/plans/bulk-approve - Bulk approve plans
const bulkApproveSchema = z.object({
  planIds: z.array(z.string()).min(1),
});

ru.post("/sweep/plans/bulk-approve", zValidator("json", bulkApproveSchema), async (c) => {
  const { planIds } = c.req.valid("json");
  const user = c.get("user") || "anonymous";

  await Promise.all(planIds.map(id => approveSweepPlan(id, user)));

  return c.json({ success: true, approved: planIds.length });
});

// GET /ru/sweep/plans/:planId - Get plan details
ru.get("/sweep/plans/:planId", async (c) => {
  const { planId } = c.req.param();

  const plan = await getSweepPlan(planId);

  if (!plan) {
    return c.json({ error: "Plan not found" }, 404);
  }

  return c.json(plan);
});

// ==================== Status/Health ====================

// GET /ru/status - Quick status for dashboard
ru.get("/status", async (c) => {
  const [fleetStats, activeSyncs, activeSweeps] = await Promise.all([
    getFleetStats(),
    getActiveSyncCount(),
    getActiveSweepCount(),
  ]);

  return c.json({
    fleet: fleetStats,
    activeSyncs,
    activeSweeps,
    healthy: fleetStats.healthy === fleetStats.cloned,
  });
});

export default ru;
```

### 2. Route Registration

```typescript
// apps/gateway/src/routes/index.ts

import { Hono } from "hono";
import { dcg } from "./dcg";
import { ru } from "./ru";
// ... other routes

const app = new Hono();

app.route("/dcg", dcg);
app.route("/ru", ru);
// ... other routes

export default app;
```

### 3. Error Handling Middleware

```typescript
// apps/gateway/src/middleware/error-handler.ts

import { Hono } from "hono";
import { NotFoundError, ConflictError, ValidationError } from "../errors";
import { logger } from "../services/logger";

export function errorHandler() {
  return async (c, next) => {
    try {
      await next();
    } catch (error) {
      const correlationId = c.get("correlationId") || generateCorrelationId();

      if (error instanceof NotFoundError) {
        logger.warn({ correlationId, error: error.message }, "Resource not found");
        return c.json({ error: error.message }, 404);
      }

      if (error instanceof ConflictError) {
        logger.warn({ correlationId, error: error.message }, "Conflict error");
        return c.json({ error: error.message }, 409);
      }

      if (error instanceof ValidationError) {
        logger.warn({ correlationId, error: error.message, details: error.details }, "Validation error");
        return c.json({ error: error.message, details: error.details }, 400);
      }

      logger.error({ correlationId, error }, "Unhandled error");
      return c.json({ error: "Internal server error" }, 500);
    }
  };
}
```

- `apps/gateway/src/routes/ru.ts` - RU route handlers

### Unit Tests (`apps/gateway/tests/unit/ru-routes.test.ts`)

```typescript
describe("RU Routes", () => {
  describe("Fleet Management", () => {
    it("GET /ru/fleet should list repos with pagination", async () => {
      await seedFleetRepos(25);

      const response = await app.request("/ru/fleet?limit=10");
      const body = await response.json();

      expect(response.status).toBe(200);
      expect(body.repos).toHaveLength(10);
      expect(body.total).toBe(25);
      expect(body.hasMore).toBe(true);

      logger.info({
        testName: "list_fleet_pagination",
        total: body.total,
        returned: body.repos.length,
        correlationId: getCorrelationId(),
      }, "Fleet listing with pagination");
    });

    it("POST /ru/fleet should add repo", async () => {
      const response = await app.request("/ru/fleet", {
        method: "POST",
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        body: JSON.stringify({
          owner: "test-org",
          name: "test-repo",
          url: "https://github.com/test-org/test-repo.git",
        }),
      });

      const body = await response.json();

      expect(response.status).toBe(201);
      expect(body.repo.fullName).toBe("test-org/test-repo");

      logger.info({
        testName: "add_repo",
        repoId: body.repo.id,
        correlationId: getCorrelationId(),
      }, "Repo added via API");
    });

    it("DELETE /ru/fleet/:repoId should remove repo", async () => {
      const repo = await addRepoToFleet({
        owner: "test",
        name: "delete-me",
        url: "https://github.com/test/delete-me.git",
      });

      const response = await app.request(`/ru/fleet/${repo.id}`, {
        method: "DELETE",
      });

      expect(response.status).toBe(200);

      const check = await app.request(`/ru/fleet/${repo.id}`);
      expect(check.status).toBe(404);

      logger.info({
        testName: "delete_repo",
        repoId: repo.id,
        correlationId: getCorrelationId(),
      }, "Repo deleted via API");
    });
  });

  describe("Sync Operations", () => {
    it("POST /ru/sync should start sync and return session ID", async () => {
      await seedFleetRepos(5);

      const response = await app.request("/ru/sync", {
        method: "POST",
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        body: JSON.stringify({ parallelism: 2 }),
      });

      const body = await response.json();

      expect(response.status).toBe(202);
      expect(body.sessionId).toMatch(/^sync_/);

      logger.info({
        testName: "start_sync",
        sessionId: body.sessionId,
        correlationId: getCorrelationId(),
      }, "Sync started via API");
    });

    it("POST /ru/sync/:sessionId/cancel should cancel sync", async () => {
      const { sessionId } = await startFleetSync("test");

      const response = await app.request(`/ru/sync/${sessionId}/cancel`, {
        method: "POST",
      });

      expect(response.status).toBe(200);

      logger.info({
        testName: "cancel_sync",
        sessionId,
        correlationId: getCorrelationId(),
      }, "Sync cancelled via API");
    });
  });

  describe("Agent Sweep", () => {
    it("POST /ru/sweep should start sweep session", async () => {
      await seedFleetRepos(3);

      const response = await app.request("/ru/sweep", {
        method: "POST",
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        body: JSON.stringify({
          targetRepos: "*",
          dryRun: true,
        }),
      });

      const body = await response.json();

      expect(response.status).toBe(202);
      expect(body.session.id).toMatch(/^sweep_/);
      expect(body.session.repoCount).toBe(3);

      logger.info({
        testName: "start_sweep",
        sessionId: body.session.id,
        repoCount: body.session.repoCount,
        correlationId: getCorrelationId(),
      }, "Sweep started via API");
    });

    it("POST /ru/sweep/plans/:planId/approve should approve plan", async () => {
      const plan = await createTestSweepPlan();

      const response = await app.request(`/ru/sweep/plans/${plan.id}/approve`, {
        method: "POST",
      });

      expect(response.status).toBe(200);

      const updated = await getSweepPlan(plan.id);
      expect(updated.approvalStatus).toBe("approved");

      logger.info({
        testName: "approve_plan",
        planId: plan.id,
        correlationId: getCorrelationId(),
      }, "Plan approved via API");
    });

    it("POST /ru/sweep/plans/:planId/reject should reject with reason", async () => {
      const plan = await createTestSweepPlan();

      const response = await app.request(`/ru/sweep/plans/${plan.id}/reject`, {
        method: "POST",
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        body: JSON.stringify({ reason: "Too risky" }),
      });

      expect(response.status).toBe(200);

      const updated = await getSweepPlan(plan.id);
      expect(updated.approvalStatus).toBe("rejected");
      expect(updated.rejectedReason).toBe("Too risky");

      logger.info({
        testName: "reject_plan",
        planId: plan.id,
        reason: "Too risky",
        correlationId: getCorrelationId(),
      }, "Plan rejected via API");
    });
  });

  describe("Validation", () => {
    it("should reject invalid repo URL", async () => {
      const response = await app.request("/ru/fleet", {
        method: "POST",
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        body: JSON.stringify({
          owner: "test",
          name: "repo",
          url: "not-a-url",
        }),
      });

      expect(response.status).toBe(400);

      logger.info({
        testName: "validation_url",
        correlationId: getCorrelationId(),
      }, "Invalid URL rejected");
    });

    it("should reject missing required fields", async () => {
      const response = await app.request("/ru/fleet", {
        method: "POST",
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        body: JSON.stringify({
          owner: "test",
          // missing name and url
        }),
      });

      expect(response.status).toBe(400);

      logger.info({
        testName: "validation_required",
        correlationId: getCorrelationId(),
      }, "Missing fields rejected");
    });
  });
});
```

### E2E Tests (`apps/gateway/tests/e2e/ru-api.test.ts`)

```typescript
describe("RU API E2E", () => {
  it("should complete full fleet management workflow", async () => {
    // Add repo
    const addResponse = await fetch("/ru/fleet", {
      method: "POST",
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      body: JSON.stringify({
        owner: "e2e-org",
        name: "e2e-repo",
        url: "https://github.com/e2e-org/e2e-repo.git",
      }),
    });
    expect(addResponse.status).toBe(201);
    const { repo } = await addResponse.json();

    // Get repo
    const getResponse = await fetch(`/ru/fleet/${repo.id}`);
    expect(getResponse.status).toBe(200);

    // Update repo
    const updateResponse = await fetch(`/ru/fleet/${repo.id}`, {
      method: "PATCH",
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      body: JSON.stringify({ group: "e2e-group" }),
    });
    expect(updateResponse.status).toBe(200);

    // Delete repo
    const deleteResponse = await fetch(`/ru/fleet/${repo.id}`, {
      method: "DELETE",
    });
    expect(deleteResponse.status).toBe(200);

    logger.info({
      testName: "e2e_fleet_workflow",
      correlationId: getCorrelationId(),
    }, "E2E fleet workflow completed");
  });

  it("should complete sweep approval workflow", async () => {
    // Start sweep
    const sweepResponse = await fetch("/ru/sweep", {
      method: "POST",
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      body: JSON.stringify({
        targetRepos: "*",
        dryRun: true,
        autoApprove: false,
      }),
    });
    expect(sweepResponse.status).toBe(202);
    const { session } = await sweepResponse.json();

    // Wait for plans to be generated
    await waitFor(async () => {
      const plansResponse = await fetch(`/ru/sweep/${session.id}/plans`);
      const { plans } = await plansResponse.json();
      return plans.length > 0;
    }, 30000);

    // Get pending plans
    const plansResponse = await fetch(`/ru/sweep/${session.id}/plans?status=pending`);
    const { plans } = await plansResponse.json();

    // Approve first plan
    if (plans.length > 0) {
      const approveResponse = await fetch(`/ru/sweep/plans/${plans[0].id}/approve`, {
        method: "POST",
      });
      expect(approveResponse.status).toBe(200);
    }

    logger.info({
      testName: "e2e_sweep_approval",
      sessionId: session.id,
      planCount: plans.length,
      correlationId: getCorrelationId(),
    }, "E2E sweep approval workflow completed");
  });
});
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Fleet CRUD endpoints work correctly
- [ ] Fleet filtering by status/group/owner works
- [ ] Fleet import from GitHub works
- [ ] Sync start returns session ID immediately
- [ ] Sync cancellation stops active operations
- [ ] Sweep session creation works
- [ ] Plan approval/rejection endpoints work
- [ ] Bulk approval works for multiple plans
- [ ] All validation errors return 400 with details
- [ ] All not-found errors return 404
- [ ] All unit tests pass with comprehensive logging
- [ ] All E2E tests pass

 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-jp1","title":"EPIC: Phase 2 - Core Features","description":"## Overview
Phase 2 adds multi-agent coordination capabilities, state management, and account management for production use.

## Phase 2 Goal
Multi-agent coordination and state management

## Key Deliverables

### Additional Agent Drivers
- ACP Agent Driver (Agent Client Protocol)
  - JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio
  - IDE integration compatibility
  - Protocol adapter for different agents

### Agent Communication
- Agent Mail integration (MCP client)
  - Messaging between agents
  - Thread management
  - Inbox/outbox operations
- File reservation system
  - Advisory locks with TTL
  - Exclusive/shared modes
  - Reservation map UI component

### Conflict Management
- Conflict detection baseline
  - File conflict detection
  - WebSocket events for conflicts
  - Alert generation

### State Management
- Checkpoint/restore system
  - Delta-based progressive checkpointing
  - Full checkpoint every 5th
  - Compression with zstd
  - Bounded restore time
- Auto-Healing Context Window Management
  - Graduated thresholds (warning 75%, critical 85%, emergency 95%)
  - Proactive summarization
  - Seamless agent rotation with context transfer
- Context pack builder
  - Token budgeting
  - Source prioritization
  - Overflow handling

### Operations
- flywheel_gateway-76h: Supervisor & Daemon Management [P2]
- Job orchestration for long-running operations
  - Context builds, scans, exports
  - Progress events via WebSocket
  - Job status endpoints
- History tracking system
  - Prompt/response summaries
  - Token usage tracking
  - Outcome recording

### Reliability
- Idempotency middleware
  - Idempotency key header
  - Response caching
  - Duplicate request handling

### Account Management
- CAAM integration (BYOA + BYOK)
  - Profile vault management
  - Account rotation on limits
  - Usage tracking per profile
  - Health monitoring

## Phase Completion Criteria
- [ ] Multiple agents can coordinate via Agent Mail
- [ ] File reservations prevent edit conflicts
- [ ] Conflicts detected and alert generated
- [ ] Checkpoints created automatically with deltas
- [ ] Restore from checkpoint works within 200ms
- [ ] Context window auto-heals before hitting limit
- [ ] Long-running operations show progress
- [ ] BYOA accounts rotate on rate limit
- [ ] All operations are idempotent

## Testing Requirements
- Unit test coverage >80% for all new services
- Integration tests for Agent Mail operations
- Integration tests for checkpoint/restore cycle
- E2E tests for multi-agent coordination scenarios
- Performance tests for checkpoint restoration time
- Load tests for context window management
- Structured test logging + artifact capture per `flywheel_gateway-d8b`

### Logging
- [ ] Unit/integration tests emit structured logs with `correlationId` + relevant entity IDs (never secrets)
- [ ] E2E tests capture artifacts (trace/video/screenshot/console logs) and surface failure context per `flywheel_gateway-d8b`


## Success Criteria

- [ ] Multi-agent coordination works end-to-end: Agent Mail integration + reservations + conflict alerts + checkpoints + context packs
- [ ] Auto-healing context window management demonstrated (thresholds, compaction/rotation, safe handoff)
- [ ] Long-running operations return job IDs + progress events; jobs are observable and cancelable
- [ ] Idempotency middleware prevents duplicated side effects for mutating endpoints
- [ ] BYOA-gated execution is enforced (no account → actionable error with recovery hints)
- [ ] Tests: unit + integration + E2E coverage for new UX flows and failure modes per `flywheel_gateway-d8b`

## Reference

- `flywheel_gateway-y19` (embedded `docs/PLAN.md` spec snapshot)

","notes":"## Constituent Beads

This EPIC encompasses the following beads:

### Additional Agent Drivers
- flywheel_gateway-o54: ACP Agent Driver [P2]

### Operations

### Reliability
